\ Executive Board Member*

Blast from the Past

[Image description: Black and white photo of a group of five young men and one adult man (Ingemar Jansson) posing outdoors. The team members are wearing matching light-colored shirts with "METRO CHAMP 78-79-80" printed on one of them. Ingemar is standing on the far right holding up a large trophy.]
Washington Interscholastic Orienteering League team winners Nathan Hale, circa 1980. COC member Ingemar Jansson presented the trophy, which he holds in the photo.

Cascade Members Excel at Mt. Dufferin Rogaine

[Image description: Photo of Jennifer and Steve Knowles standing outdoors against a natural backdrop, smiling.]
Jennifer and Steve Knowles (left) won the mixed division at the recent Mt. Dufferin 6-hour Rogaine held in Kamloops, British Columbia. Eric Bone and Bill Cusworth won the men's division.

Cascade Successfully Loses Cascadia Cup

[Image description: Photo of Martin Pardoe and Marie-Catherine Bruno smiling excitedly while holding a large, ornate wooden trophy shaped like a chalice. Martin is holding a blue sports drink.]
Canadians Martin Pardoe and Marie-Catherine Bruno celebarte [sic] their Cascadia Cup victory.

In a brilliant display of creative scoring, Rick Breseman led the charge to return the Cascadia Cup to Canada. Inspired by his desire to get the cup out of his house, where it has been since the last Cascadia Cup, Rick figured out a way for the badly-outnumbered Canadian team to head home with the cup. It wasn't a total loss, though: inside the cup was a bottle of beer.

New O' Suits Have Arrived

[Image description: Photo of seven orienteers, both adults and youth, posing closely together outdoors on a grassy field. They are all wearing matching brand-new Trimtex orienteering suits featuring a blue, yellow, white, and green design.]

The new club O’ suits are here! Cascade OC received its snappy new Trimtex orienteering suits from Scarborough Orienteering. If you ordered one, you can pick it up from Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). We will probably be placing another order sometime in the fall, so if you didn't order one, you will have another chance then.

Rooks Park - Walla Walla, WA

Sat, May 4, 2002 (starts 3-4 pm)
Sun, May 5, 2002 (starts 9-11 am)
Sacajawea Orienteers
Course Setter & Meet Director: Frank Skorina
Control Pickup: Michel Billaux, Kally Rapp, Matt Skorina
Saturday Participants: 7
Sunday Participants: 12

WaHi Freshman PE instructor Gayle See, gives an easy opportunity to earn 100 extra credit points — attend an orienteering meet. Five hardy students (and two Moms) took Mrs. See up on her offer (although I was told that many more could have used the extra credit). The weather turned out to be nice (albeit windy) but hard Sunday morning rain almost scared me off. Turn out was lower than expected for this National Orienteering Day meet, perhaps due in part to the newspaper not running the press release that I submitted.

Our next meet is May 11-12 at Sacajawea State Park in Pasco. I will promote this meet as a National Orienteering Day meet.

[Image description: Black and white photo of a group of five young men and one adult man (Ingemar Jansson) posing outdoors. The team members are wearing matching light-colored shirts with "METRO CHAMP 78-79-80" printed on one of them. Ingemar is standing on the far right holding up a large trophy.] Washington Interscholastic Orienteering League team winners Nathan Hale, circa 1980. COC member Ingemar Jansson presented the trophy, which he holds in the photo.
Cascade Members Excel at Mt. Dufferin Rogaine

[Image description: Photo of Jennifer and Steve Knowles standing outdoors against a natural backdrop, smiling.] Jennifer and Steve Knowles (left) won the mixed division at the recent Mt. Dufferin 6-hour Rogaine held in Kamloops, British Columbia. Eric Bone and Bill Cusworth won the men's division.

[Image description: Photo of Martin Pardoe and Marie-Catherine Bruno smiling excitedly while holding a large, ornate wooden trophy shaped like a chalice. Martin is holding a blue sports drink.] Canadians Martin Pardoe and Marie-Catherine Bruno celebarte [sic] their Cascadia Cup victory.

In a brilliant display of creative scoring, Rick Breseman led the charge to return the Cascadia Cup to Canada. Inspired by his desire to get the cup out of his house, where it has been since the last Cascadia Cup, Rick figured out a way for the badly-outnumbered Canadian team to head home with the cup. It wasn't a total loss, though: inside the cup was a bottle of beer.

[Image description: Photo of seven orienteers, both adults and youth, posing closely together outdoors on a grassy field. They are all wearing matching brand-new Trimtex orienteering suits featuring a blue, yellow, white, and green design.]

The new club O’ suits are here! Cascade OC received its snappy new Trimtex orienteering suits from Scarborough Orienteering. If you ordered one, you can pick it up from Jennifer Knowles (253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com). We will probably be placing another order sometime in the fall, so if you didn't order one, you will have another chance then.

(24 controls, any order, 1 hour time limit, 2-control/minute overtime penalty)

Sat, May 4, 2002 (starts 3-4 pm) Sun, May 5, 2002 (starts 9-11 am) Sacajawea Orienteers Course Setter & Meet Director: Frank Skorina Control Pickup: Michel Billaux, Kally Rapp, Matt Skorina Saturday Participants: 7 Sunday Participants: 12

WaHi Freshman PE instructor Gayle See, gives an easy opportunity to earn 100 extra credit points — attend an orienteering meet. Five hardy students (and two Moms) took Mrs. See up on her offer (although I was told that many more could have used the extra credit). The weather turned out to be nice (albeit windy) but hard Sunday morning rain almost scared me off. Turn out was lower than expected for this National Orienteering Day meet, perhaps due in part to the newspaper not running the press release that I submitted.
Our next meet is May 11-12 at Sacajawea State Park in Pasco. I will promote this meet as a National Orienteering Day meet.
Allison camp, Lia Prins, Rachel Elia
